evidence.title: README (segment 1)
evidence.excerpt: Know your quota before you hit the wall. A statusline for Claude Code — single Bash file, zero npm.

evidence.title: README (segment 2)
evidence.excerpt: Most statuslines show "you used 60%." That number means nothing without context. 60% with 30 minutes left? Fine, the window resets soon. 60% with 4 hours left? You're about to hit the wall. claude-lens compares your usage rate to the time remaining and shows the delta. No Node.js, no npm, no lock files. Single Bash file.

evidence.title: README (segment 3)
evidence.excerpt: - **⇣15%** green = you've used 15% less than expected. Headroom. Keep going. - **⇡15%** red = you're burning 15% faster than sustainable. Slow down. - **15%** / **20%** = used in the 5h and 7d windows. **3h** = resets in 3 hours. - Top line: model, effort, project `(branch)`, `3f +24 -7` = git diff stats

evidence.title: README (segment 4)
evidence.excerpt: ```bash claude plugin marketplace add Astro-Han/claude-lens claude plugin install claude-lens ```

evidence.title: README (segment 7)
evidence.excerpt: | | claude-lens | Node.js/TypeScript statuslines | Rust/Go statuslines | |---|---|---|---| | Runtime | `jq` | Node.js 18+ / npm | Compiled binary | | Codebase | Single file | 1000+ lines + node_modules | Compiled, not inspectable | | Execution | ~10ms, 3% of refresh cycle | ~90ms, 30% of refresh cycle | ~5ms (est.) | | Memory | ~2 MB | ~57 MB | ~3 MB (est.) | | Failure modes | Read-only, worst case prints "Claude" | Runtime dependency, package manager | Generally stable | | Pace tracking | Usage rate vs time remaining | Trend-only or none | None |

evidence.title: README (segment 8)
evidence.excerpt: Execution and memory measured on Apple Silicon, 300 runs, same stdin JSON. Rust/Go values are estimates.

evidence.title: Add option for reporting usage, not remaining values
evidence.excerpt: Add option for reporting usage, not remaining values (state:closed)

evidence.title: v0.6.0
evidence.excerpt: ## What's Changed - Display usage as **used%** instead of remaining% (lower = better) - Use ⇡/⇣ arrows for pace delta trend (⇡ = overspend, ⇣ = surplus) - Invert pace delta sign to match intuitive convention - Color constants for cleaner code **Full Changelog**: https://github.com/Astro-Han/claude-lens/compare/v0.5.0...v0.6.0
